mnemonics: My Greedy Pal Always Picks Largest (MGPAAPL): Makes Greedy Problems, Always Pick Largest. Stands for the core idea: Make a choice that seems best at the moment (greedy), Prove its safety (often with an exchange argument), Assemble the solution iteratively, and Pick the Largest/smallest/most optimal local option.
keyPoints: The Greedy Method makes a series of choices, each of which looks best at the moment (a locally optimal choice) in the hope that it will lead to a globally optimal solution.
It is applicable to optimization problems where a greedy choice property and optimal substructure are present. Not all problems can be solved greedily.

Common examples include: Activity Selection (pick the activity that ends earliest), Huffman Coding (merge the two least frequent nodes), and Fractional Knapsack (take items with the highest value/weight ratio first).
commonMistakes: Assuming a greedy solution is always optimal without proving it. A greedy approach must be proven correct for the specific problem, often using an exchange argument.
Confusing the Greedy Method with Dynamic Programming. Greedy makes an irrevocable choice and never reconsiders, while DP explores all possibilities and combines results.
